Based on the letter dated March 21, 2025, the agency has asserted that the materials shown in the above-pasted pictures are owned by the Department of Education (DepEd) School District, cut down from the mahogany trees planted in Cayam High School for the purpose of clearing the area to give room for the construction of a new building. They added that the lumber from the mahogany trees will be utilized for the improvement of bleachers once the major repair of grandstand will be accomplished.
As for the Galvanized Iron Sheets (G.I. Sheets), the agency reasoned that such pertains to the removed and replaced sheets due to holes causing the rain to drip on the bleachers.
Without any attestation from the proper authorities of DepEd confirming their ownership over the subject construction materials, the team stands by their recommendation.

The audit team acknowledges receipt of the aforementioned response. We recommended and the management agreed that the LDRRMO refrain from spending on programs, projects and activities not included in the ADRRMFIP. We recommended and the management agreed that the HRMO assess whether the tasks assigned to JO workers are genuinely temporary or should be performed by permanent employees. We recommended and the management agreed to consider the creation of the LYDO position so that it will be headed by a regular employee rather than hiring JOs, otherwise, if there is fund constraint and PS limitation is exceeded in case said office will be created, designate existing personnel who is deemed fit to serve this purpose until such time that the local government unit can already create this office.

We recommended that management submit an explanation and/or justification why the subject disbursement did not undergo the required procedures necessary in a negotiated procurement for a commissioned service of an artist (i.e., choreographer), in accordance with Section 7.a    and 7.b, Annex H of the IRR of RA 9184.

In a letter dated March 15, 2025, the management has informed that the reason for the above-referred observation is the delay in the release of financial assistance from the Provincial Government of Bohol (PGBh), the grantor of the fund. The agency added that it was only on July 23, 2024 when the corresponding check of the financial assistance was released and the actual competition is to be held four days after such receipt – July 27, 2024. As such, the delay prompted the management to temporarily shoulder the cost using personal funds, with the intent to reimburse it once the agency has received the financial assistance.
The rationale presented by the agency remains unpersuasive. It has been determined that a contract with the engaged choreographer was formalized on July 10, 2024. Ideally, prior to this date, the agency should have prepared and executed the required procedures provided in our procurement law in engaging services pertaining to scientific, scholarly, or artistic works notwithstanding the delay in the release of the financial assistance.
It is an established practice in requesting financial assistance to secure first a Memorandum of Agreement/Understanding signed by the grantor and grantee (the PGBh and the agency in this case), as well as an approved Activity Design. Through these documents, the agency could have impliedly relied on the certainty of the funds and could have proceeded with the appropriate steps to be undertaken in a negotiated procurement. Further, the payment of services rendered is normally done at the end of the contract, which in this case was the actual date of the performance, July 27 and by which point, the funds were already received by the LGU.

We recommended that management stop the practice of pre-signing unissued accountable forms including AF No. 51 to avoid exposure of government resources to possible misuse or loss.

In a letter dated February 25, 2025, the agency has informed the team that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office has stopped the practice of pre-signing unissued accountable forms, immediately after such observation was brought up by the audit team.

The audit team acknowledges receipt of the aforementioned response. However, pending proper validation of the agency's assertion regarding corrective action, the team maintains by its recommendation. As validation procedures necessitate more than the mere submission of documentation, it is expected that such procedures will be conducted at the earliest opportune time, specifically during fieldwork.

We recommend the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation for the depreciable component of the road network and flood control system following the straight-line method without provision for residual value.

We recommend the management to provide the audit team a copy of the Designation Order of the new acting HRMO. Henceforth, management is recommended to be always aware of the timeliness of the appointment of personnel in acting capacity status so as not to disrupt the services of the official to the constituents.

We recommend that the management submit the copy of the declaration of province-wide state of calamity to support the obligation of the LDRRMF for purposes other than those prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002; and to strictly utilize LDRRMF only for its intended purposes only.

We recommend that management submit the LDRRMFIP as incorporated in a Supplemental Investment Plan approved by the Local Sanggunian to support the various expenditures charged to the Unspent LDRRMF of the prior years in consonance to Section 5.1.2 and 5.1.3.2 of COA Circular 2012-002.
We recommend the management to pay the monthly RATA only after the end of the month to establish correct payments according to the services rendered as required in DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to ensure the timely implementation of all 20% Development Projects by instructing the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of the projects, and at the same time, to provide specific target dates of implementation in the Revised/ Annual Investment Plan (AIP).

We recommend the management to demand from the concerned contractors retention money or pertinent warranty security considering the release of these money before final acceptance. Henceforth, release of the 10% retention money on completed infrastructure projects has to be only upon substitution of the retention money by an equivalent amount of “on demand” bank guarantee acceptable to and duly confirmed by the agency, or upon final acceptance of the works after the expiration of the applicable defects-liability period. To ensure that the warranty security is posted, the LGU may make it as a requirement before returning the 10% retention money.

observations.recommendation:
RECOMMENDATION

      We recommend that the  Municipal  Treasurer  require  all  accountable
 officers/collectors to remit/turn over all collections daily.  Furthermore,
 instruct the Municipal  Treasurer  to  properly  control  and  monitor  the
 issuance of accountable forms to the Revenue collection clerks. She has  to
 see to  it  that  collectors  will  not  be  given  additional  booklet  of
 accountable forms unless the proceeds of the previous ones  given to   them
 were remitted. We further recommend that sanctions have to  be imposed,  if
 necessary.

RECOMMENDATION


       We recommend that  management  designate  proper  office/official  to
control and monitor the issuance and approval of the Driver’s Trip  Ticket
as  well  as  the   authorization   of   withdrawal   of   fuel   from   the
refilling/gasoline station.


      Further, we recommend the Municipal Accountant to verify that payments
of fuel of heavy equipments are not paid twice and for purposes of  projects
not under contract.

RECOMMENDATION

      We  recommend  the  management  to   comply   with   the   documentary
requirements for repairs, and maintain records/Subsidiary Ledger of  repairs
 for each property. Furthermore,  require the Municipal Accountant  to   see
to  it  that  all  claims  for  repairs    are   supported   with   complete
documentation to ensure  validity  and  propriety  of  government  funds  as
required under existing regulations.

RECOMMENDATION

      We recommend that management direct the Property Officer together with
the Disposal Committee  to  process  the  documents/forms  for  disposal  of
unserviceable properties and submit to  the  COA  Auditor  pursuant  to  the
Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, and Section 79 of PD  No.
1445.

      Further, we recommend that the Municipal  Accountant,  upon  disposal,
drop the cost of the unserviceable/obsolete/unfound PPE from  the  books  to
reflect the accurate value of PPE in the financial statements.

RECOMMENDATION

      We recommend that management require  the  Property  Officer/Municipal
Treasurer to maintain Property Cards  for  all  properties  of  the  LGU  as
required  under  Section  45  of   the  NGAS  Manual  for  LGUs  Volume  II.
Henceforth, require inventory committee/team  to  immediately  conduct   the
physical inventory of all properties and  submit  the  corresponding  Report
on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment  to  the  auditor  as
well as the reconciliation of  records  maintained  by  the  Office  of  the
Municipal Accountant and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.

RECOMMENDATION

      We recommend that management require the Local Finance  Committee  and
the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare  a  budget  based  on  a  reasonable
estimate of income so as not to incur overdraft or loss in its waterworks
operations.  Henceforth, limit total disbursements to  fifty  percent  (50%)
of the uncollected estimated revenue accruing to the  Fund  in  addition  to
the actual collections, provided that no cash overdraft shall  be  incurred,
pursuant to Section 158 of the Government  Accounting  and  Auditing  Manual
(GAAM), Volume I, in order not to incur net loss of current income.

RECOMMENDATION

      We   recommend   that   management   provide   resources    for    the
revision/updating of the Local Revenue Code in accordance with Republic  Act
7160  or  the  Local  Government  Code  of  1991  and  coordinate  with  the
Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG)  and  the  Department  of
Finance (DOF) through the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF)  in  the
proper imposition of taxes and fees in accordance with  Joint  Circular  No.
2019-01.

RECOMMENDATION


      We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Treasurer  be  required  to  make
representation to the Bureau of Internal  Revenue  for  the  computation  of
taxes due them from the exercise of corporate power in the LGU’s   Operation
of Roxas Park Facilities.

Henceforth, project implementors have to see  to it that in the implementation of future  projects, the design standards has to be followed to ensure the quality and safety of the road users.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the overstatement of Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax receivables to reflect its accurate balance. This is in view of the need to record in the books actual receivables from said taxes in order to reflect accurate amounts for the RPT and SET receivables.

Recommendation:
We recommend the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to improve the collection efficiency of the LGU by conducting tax campaign and information drive in order to encourage more tax payers to pay taxes on time.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipality establish the Local Council for the Protection of Children and allocate 1% of its IRA for the programs, projects and activities for the protection of children and youth in accordance with the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2002-121 and Section 15 of Republic Act 9344.

Recommendation
We recommend the management to comply with the documentary requirements for repairs, and maintain records/Subsidiary Ledger of repairs  for each property. Furthermore,  require the Municipal Accountant to  see to it that all claims for repairs  are supported with complete documentation to ensure that government funds are expended for  proper and valid expenses as required under existing regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require all officials and employees concerned and responsible in the preparation, approving of trip tickets as well as authorized passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip. Also, require the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Fuel Consumption Report for each vehicle and  submit the same to the auditor.
Furthermore, everytime the vehicle/heavy equipment shall be refueled at the refilling station, a checker/inspector have to accompany and check whether the correct quantity of fuel being issued is the same as that pumped into the vehicle or heavy equipment.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management have to see to it that disbursements are supported with complete documentary requirements before payment is effected as required under Section 4.6 of Presidential Decree 1445 in order to establish the propriety and validity of transaction.

Recommendation
We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms as required and submit it to the Auditor to serve as guide in determination and reconciliation of accountabilities and to establish actual collection.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management intensify the implementation and monitoring of municipal projects to deliver goods and timely services to the constituents.   Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor install monitoring strategies on the prioritization of its projects’ implementation.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of Section 271(d) of Republic Act 7160 to facilitate the release of these funds to the barangays to avoid the accumulation of trust funds in the municipal treasury and hamper the implementation of the barangay projects programmed during the year and the same time presenting fairly the actual liability of the local government unit and actual income of the barangay. The immediate release of these funds to the barangay as well as the succeeding years’ share on said income within five(5) days after the end of each quarter augment its meager resources and ensure its prompt utilization.

We recommend that management appoint officials and personnel to conduct DRRM activities to assure that risk of lives and property be reduced in times of calamity and necessary and adequate disaster preparedness could be undertaken. Further, we also recommend that MDRRMO be strengthened for them to be responsive in times of calamities.

We recommend that management  closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned programs/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose is attained/achieved  as required Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18, Republic Act 10121.

We recommend that management comply with the provisions of Section 287 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and the related DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the prompt and full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the implementation of priority development projects in order to optimally attain the socio-economic and environmental development of the municipality. Henceforth, utilize all balances of the 20% Development Fund continuing appropriations for the implementation of development projects in order to attain the intended purpose for which the fund was appropriated.

We recommend that management  require the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of tires to its appropriate inventory account using the perpetual inventory method pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Manual Volume I. Henceforth, require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit it to the Municipal Accountant which shall be used as basis in recording the issuances/expense account as required under Section 121 of the same manual.

We recommend that management  limit the cash advance for salaries and wages to the net amount of payroll for a pay period and  the cash advance voucher should be supported with copies of approved payroll showing the net amount of payroll for the pay period as basis of the amount to be drawn pursuant to Section 4.2.1 & 4.2..2 of COA Circular 97-002.  Further, these should be liquidated within five days after each payday as required under existing regulation.

We recommend that management  closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned programs/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose is attained/achieved as required Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18 of Republic Act 10121.
